creator: ade@lovett.com
text:
On Thu, Aug 19, 1999 at 02:36:58PM +0200, Sheldon Hearn wrote:
> 
> Could you take a look at the port as it stands, with rev 1.7 of the
> Makefile, and see what needs to be done after Chris's changes?

Ok.  I have slain the beast, and got a working port.

1. Remove the following files:
	patches/patch-ab
	patches/patch-ac
	patches/patch-ad
	patches/patch-ae
	patches/patch-af
	patches/patch-ag

2. Extract the attached tarball to give three files:

	patch-ah and patch-ai	-> patches/
	upgrade			diff to take port to 0.28.1

The key point to a successful build is that this code absolutely
requires gcc-2.95.1 (not even gcc-2.95 -- that worked under -stable,
but not -current).  Not being a C++ person, I don't fully understand the
ramifications, however I'm aware that C++ support has always been
'a little odd' with various versions of gcc/egcs.

As and when gcc-2.95.1 becomes the base compiler for -current (with
the appropriate change to OSVERSION), the (hopefully obvious) comments
in the Makefile can be reverted - by that time we should have the
USE_NEWGCC switch in bsd.port.mk to make things a little clearer.
